institutional_asymmetry_pattern: Public register row has supplier, governance, and bilingual exact-row history exports while buyer-side procurement and public assessment attachments remain absent.
strongest_visible_public_signal: Named supplier plus human-oversight and impact-assessment signals on a public municipal register surface.
primary_gap_now: No public buyer-side tender/award/contract or downloadable DPIA/ethical-review companion is locally exposed for the named ChatAmsterdam row.
reviewer_safe_claim_now: The Amsterdam row is still the richest current LIMEN register-style disclosure surface because the public register names supplier and governance/assessment fields, but the captured route set does not yet expose buyer-side or publicly inspectable companion-document support.
forbidden_overread: Do not caption Amsterdam as procurement-absent, contract-resolved, implementation-verified, or public-assessment-complete.
paper_thesis_use: Best Figure 1B / Table 1B anchor for showing that high machine-readable disclosure does not collapse the procurement gap.
figure_table_hook: Traceability ladder; named-assessment-vs-public-inspectability badge; Amsterdam blocker card.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer a genuinely different static official procurement or public assessment route for Amsterdam; do not repeat blocked municipal search or the same static TenderNed shell probes.

institutional_asymmetry_pattern: Public register row plus same-buyer supplier-consistent award metadata exists, but exact page-specific contract and public assessment linkage remain unproven.
strongest_visible_public_signal: Register page plus Hilma award notice 2021-062296 naming Oy IBM Finland Ab with EUR 270992.0.
primary_gap_now: The public procurement package is same-buyer and supplier-consistent, but it still does not identify the parking-chatbot page itself or attach a page-specific DPIA/impact-assessment document.
reviewer_safe_claim_now: The Helsinki row sits above register-only disclosure because an official buyer-side procurement package and IBM-consistent award metadata are public, but the linkage remains family-level rather than exact row-specific proof.
forbidden_overread: Do not caption Helsinki as exact contract-linked procurement proof, page-specific award proof, or public DPIA/impact-assessment completeness.
paper_thesis_use: Best middle-state exemplar for distinguishing family-level procurement continuity from exact row-to-contract proof.
figure_table_hook: Traceability ladder endpoint; family-level-linkage badge; supplier-consistency field.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Freeze Helsinki unless a page-specific contract, tender lot, DPIA, or impact-assessment document explicitly tied to the parking-chatbot row appears.

institutional_asymmetry_pattern: Direct regulator notice yields strong legal-basis and remedy text while buyer-side procurement and supplier-chain visibility remain absent.
strongest_visible_public_signal: ICO notice PDF text with Articles 5(1)(a), 6, 9 and Annex 1 remedy language.
primary_gap_now: The notice gives legal-basis and remedy text, but no public buyer-side procurement, supplier contract, or public impact-assessment companion is locally exposed.
reviewer_safe_claim_now: The Serco row is strong for direct regulator governance text but still weak for buyer-side traceability and procurement reconstruction.
forbidden_overread: Do not convert regulator notice text into procurement visibility, supplier proof, sector prevalence, or broad legality conclusions.
paper_thesis_use: Best main-text comparator for legal/governance depth without procurement reconstructability.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Hold until a public buyer-side acquisition or assessment companion is discoverable from official sources; do not turn the notice into procurement proof.

institutional_asymmetry_pattern: Direct regulator reprimand yields process-failure and DPIA-defect text while procurement, supplier, and attached DPIA remain absent.
strongest_visible_public_signal: ICO reprimand PDF text on Article 35(1), March 2023 deployment, and invalid opt-out consent framing.
primary_gap_now: The reprimand confirms DPIA/process-failure and deployment timing, but no public buyer-side procurement record, supplier contract, or downloadable DPIA/impact-assessment companion is locally attached.
reviewer_safe_claim_now: The Chelmer row supports narrow governance/process-failure claims from direct regulator text, not acquisition-route or publicly inspectable assessment claims.
forbidden_overread: Do not flatten reprimand text into acquisition transparency, supplier proof, or school-sector prevalence.
paper_thesis_use: Best comparator for governance-failure depth and missing public assessment attachment.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Hold until one public school procurement, supplier, or public DPIA/impact-assessment companion appears; do not infer one from the reprimand alone.

institutional_asymmetry_pattern: Official public-body page establishes project purpose and governance framing but does not expose register, procurement, supplier, or deployment-proof fields.
strongest_visible_public_signal: Official police-hosted project page with project/funding/governance language.
primary_gap_now: The current official page establishes public-body involvement and stated project framing, but no procurement identifier, supplier trace, register companion, or public technical/assessment document is locally attached.
reviewer_safe_claim_now: The ORION row supports bounded public-body-existence and project-purpose claims only; it remains below register or buyer-side traceability states.
forbidden_overread: Do not present ORION as procurement-backed deployment, legality proof, supplier-normalized reconstruction, or measured operational effectiveness.
paper_thesis_use: Best low-rung floor row for keeping project-page disclosure distinct from register and regulator surfaces.
figure_table_hook: Traceability ladder low-rung state; official-project-page caution badge.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Hold unless a public procurement/register/technical companion emerges from official surfaces; do not reuse it as procurement or deployment proof.
